meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS. Master a client-focused workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your MVP faster than ever. A beginner-level guide.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of rigid, client-facing constraints. By implementing a 'Client-Driven Workflow,' you shift focus from 'building features' to 'solving client outcomes.' Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, ensuring every commit moves you toward a paid launch.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with 'almost-finished' MVPs. You start with a surge of adrenaline, but soon you're lost in a labyrinth of scope creep, refactoring, and feature-bloat. The silence of an unlaunched product is a founder’s greatest enemy, and it stems from a lack of external accountability.

To break this cycle, you must stop being a developer and start being a product architect. By adopting a strict client-centric workflow, you force your project to solve a specific problem for a specific user. We will use Antigravity to automate your scaffolding and Claude Code to maintain velocity, ensuring your project hits the market before your motivation burns out.

steps.title: Define the Client Problem-Set
steps.description: Before writing code, document the exact pain point your client faces. This prevents feature creep by keeping your focus on the 'Must-Haves' for the first sale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Architect. Generate a lean feature set for a SaaS tool that solves [INSERT PROBLEM] for [TARGET AUDIENCE]. Prioritize features based on highest potential for recurring revenue.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a requirements.md file defining the MVP scope based on the top 3 revenue-generating features.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate. This eliminates the 'blank page' syndrome and sets up your database and auth instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack SaaS application using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include user authentication and a clean dashboard layout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure using the Antigravity scaffold and verify all environment variables are set.'

steps.title: Implement the Client Data Loop
steps.description: Build the mechanism that allows clients to input data and see immediate results. This creates the 'Aha!' moment that justifies your subscription price.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a data input form component that writes to the Supabase 'user_data' table and triggers a simulated AI analysis response.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build the input form and test the database write-read cycle for user-submitted data.'

steps.title: Automate the Revenue Gate
steps.description: Integrate a payment flow early. If your client can't pay you, you don't have a SaaS—you have a hobby. Use Stripe to gate your core functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe integration module that restricts access to the dashboard until a subscription is active.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate Stripe Checkout and update middleware to protect private routes.'

steps.title: Rapid Iteration via Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle refactoring and bug fixes while you focus on customer feedback. Keep your velocity high by offloading the grunt work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for potential bottlenecks and suggest optimizations for faster load times.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run a full audit of the codebase, fix existing linting errors, and optimize the database query performance.'

pro_tips: Never build a feature unless you have a paying user waiting for it.
Use 'Time-Boxed Sprints'—if a feature takes more than 48 hours to build, cut it down.
Deploy to production on day one, even if only the landing page exists.
Automate your feedback loop; use a simple form to collect user pain points directly in your app.
Replace 'perfect code' with 'shippable code'—you can always refactor after your first $1,000 in revenue.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus entirely on cold outreach to your first 10 users. Do not add new features until you have validated your value proposition with actual revenue.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping for profit. Your MVP is closer than you think, and the market is waiting for your solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
